brief_justification: Correctly identifies base rate neglect with a concrete P(hired|X) comparison and numerical example showing when 8/10 would be expected by chance; Goes two levels deep — names the error, provides mathematical structure, and shows what comparison is missing; Includes the injection context header but shows no metacognitive monitoring of its own reasoning process or assumption-checking mid-analysis; Verifies by disconfirming all three alternative answers with specific reasons, though no counterfactual or boundary-condition test is applied to the chosen answer; Acknowledges survivorship bias as 'tempting' but presents the final conclusion with full confidence, no explicit statement of what evidence would change the answer; Systematically addresses all four options (A, B, C, D) with specific reasons for rejection, not just dismissal; Every conclusion traces to an explicit derivation — the base rate formula, the numerical example, and the structural definitions of each wrong bias are all shown; Avoids halo-effect amplification (does not favor confirmation bias or survivorship bias despite their familiarity) but does not explicitly name the negative gate pattern or why it was avoided; Avoids both halo-effect amplification and bias blind spot in the object-level answer, but the reasoning scaffold itself was not applied metacognitively to the agent's own process; Response is linear (question → answer → elimination) despite the topology specifying a BACKGROUND_LOOP with scan → gate → interrupt → debiasing cycle structure
